commit.message:
[TrimmableTypeMap] Use JniNativeMethod for RegisterNatives bootstrap (#11002)

* Use JniNativeMethod for zero-allocation RegisterNatives bootstrap

Replace Marshal.GetDelegateForFunctionPointer wrapper with
JniNativeMethod stackalloc + ReadOnlySpan<JniNativeMethod> for the
mono.android.Runtime.registerNatives bootstrap registration.

No delegate allocation, no GC handle — same pattern as the generated
proxy RegisterNatives methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/Mono.Android/Microsoft.Android.Runtime/TrimmableTypeMap.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use JniType UTF-8 constructor ("mono/android/Runtime"u8)

Avoids string-to-UTF8 conversion at runtime. The ReadOnlySpan<byte>
overload was added in dotnet/java-interop#1393.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove RegisterMethod - already removed in a separate PR

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify RegisterNatives: collection expression, less nesting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Replace global no-car warning banner

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: smooth streaming with throttling and CJK-aware chunking

- Add smoothStream transform with Intl.Segmenter for CJK word boundaries
  and 10ms inter-chunk delays to handle Azure's chunky streaming
- Enable experimental_throttle: 50ms on frontend useChat to reduce
  re-render frequency during message streaming
- Pass isAnimating prop to MessageResponse so Streamdown animates
  new text tokens smoothly as they arrive

This addresses the rough streaming effect by buffering tokens on the
backend and throttling UI updates on the frontend, creating a more
natural reading experience especially for Asian languages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: enforce numpy<2.0 constraint during sendspin install (#109)

pip install sendspin (without --no-deps) was pulling numpy>=2.0
as a transitive dep, overriding the pin from requirements.txt.
Use -c (constraint file) to enforce numpy<2.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Simplify the global header hierarchy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: full-stack Azure OpenAI chatbot with Vercel AI SDK

Implement complete backend-frontend integration using Express + Vercel AI SDK Core + Azure OpenAI:

**Backend (new):**
- Express server on port 3001 with CORS and JSON parsing
- POST /api/chat endpoint streaming via pipeUIMessageStreamToResponse
- Azure provider with configurable endpoint, API key, and API version
- Environment configuration via .env with required template

**Frontend (refactored):**
- Replaced mock chat store with lightweight session metadata registry
- Integrated useChat hook from @ai-sdk/react for message management
- Vite dev proxy for /api → localhost:3001
- Added stop button to chat composer for stream interruption

**Monorepo improvements:**
- Root npm scripts for concurrent dev launch via concurrently
- Separate dev:backend and dev:frontend targets
- Updated README with configuration and launch instructions

**Package versions (latest stable):**
- ai@6.0.140, @ai-sdk/azure@3.0.49, @ai-sdk/react@3.0.142
- express@5.2.1, concurrently@9.2.1

**Environment setup:**
- AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT: Full endpoint URL with /openai suffix
- AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY: Azure API key
- AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION: API version (defaults to 2024-12-01-preview)
- AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME: Deployment name

Run `npm run dev` to launch both servers concurrently, or `npm run dev:backend` / `npm run dev:frontend` separately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update .github/workflows/covr.yaml

co-pilot suggests there aren't needed for the covr job - it's probably right. devtools may be needed though...

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(security): improve OpenSSF scorecard score (#163)

Improve the OpenSSF Scorecard score from 6.4/10 by addressing several
checks:

- Add ClusterFuzzLite fuzzing integration with two fuzz targets
(WebSocket message parsing, auth validation)
- Fix known vulnerabilities by upgrading picomatch, requests, and
landing/demo-video deps
- Harden branch protection: enforce_admins, strict status checks,
dismiss_stale_reviews, require_last_push_approval
- Add a gh-aw agentic workflow that monitors the scorecard weekly and
creates issues for low-scoring checks
- Recompile docs/requirements.txt to pick up latest pip dependency
versions

Closes #162

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use azure.chat() API instead of responses and fix endpoint URL format

The default azure() function uses the OpenAI Responses API (/v1/responses)
which is not supported by Azure OpenAI. Changed to azure.chat() which
properly targets the Chat Completions API.

Also fixed the endpoint URL to strip trailing slashes (was causing
double-slash in constructed URLs) and clarified that the endpoint
must include the /openai suffix.

triggered multiple initialization attempts.

rho_wall=1100, rho_fluid=1020, loss_tangent=0.25). Previously figures

[revision] Address Round 5 reviewer findings

Code fixes:
- Update AbdominalModelV2 defaults to canonical (9 params were stale v1)
- Fix loss_tangent=0.3 -> 0.25 across acoustic_coupling, multilayer_wall, organ_inclusions
- Fix stale ka=0.017 comment -> 0.0114

Paper fixes:
- Reconcile coupling ratio: 4.6e4 -> 6.6e4 throughout (abstract, intro, conclusion)
- Fix Table 3: E=0.5 MPa sphere frequencies n=3 (11.3->10.0), n=4 (14.4->12.9)
- Fix Table 4 caption: remove incorrect sqrt formula, state ratio directly
- Fix conclusion garden-path sentence (semicolon before 'and flexural')
- Fix bare 'This' pronoun -> 'This disparity'
- Add figure cross-references for fig:geometry, fig:modes, fig:sobol, fig:coupling
- Add n=1 mode explanation (rigid-body translation, zero frequency)
- Clarify UQ PIEZO threshold as pressure-based estimate

Supplementary fixes:
- Fix (1-nu) -> (1-nu^2) in k_shell denominator (0.55 -> 0.7975)
- Fix k_shell value (2.3e5 -> 1.0e5 Pa/m)
- Fix f0 (2900 -> 2500 Hz)

Test updates:
- Update 7 regression tests to canonical expected values
- Fix Ritz convergence test to use explicit parameters

All 118 tests pass. Paper compiles clean (0 errors, 0 undefined refs).

commit.message:
chore: add .vite/ to .gitignore

Vite pre-bundling cache should not be tracked in git.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[review] Reviewer B Round 5

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Firebase forEach bug — snapshot.forEach stops on truthy return

Array.push() returns the new array length (truthy), which caused
Firebase's snapshot.forEach() to stop after the first child.
Fixed by wrapping in braces to avoid implicit return.
Also removed redundant orderByChild since we sort client-side,
and simplified seed logic since Firebase is now the source of truth.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[paper] Trim abstract (244->166 words), shorten highlights (<85 chars)

Submission blockers resolved:
- Abstract reduced from 244 to 166 words (JSV limit: 200)
- All 5 highlights shortened to under 85 characters each

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[bibliography] Add 8 missing references flagged by Reviewer A (#36)

Add references strengthening broader applications and filling citation gaps:
- Gavreau1966: Original infrasound research (Acustica)
- Southall2007: Marine mammal noise exposure criteria (Aquatic Mammals)
- Ishitake2002: WBV effects on gastric motility (JSV)
- Seidel1986: Long-term WBV effects review (Int Arch Occup Environ Health)
- Mayorga1997: Primary blast overpressure pathology (Toxicology)
- vonGierke2002: Organ resonance data from Harris' Handbook ch.42
- Heil1996: Shell theory for fluid-filled biological tubes (J Fluids Struct)
- Bailey2003: Physical mechanisms of therapeutic ultrasound (Acoust Phys)

Citations placed in introduction, formulation, and discussion sections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: JSV submission readiness checklist

Audit of all 14 submission requirements for Journal of Sound and Vibration.
11/13 pass; abstract word count (246 > 200) and two highlights (>85 chars)
need trimming before upload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[audit] Paper 2 gas-pocket consistency audit — 5 critical, 5 major issues (#33)

Full parameter/numerical audit of paper2-gas-pockets/main.tex against
gas_pocket_detailed.py and gas_pocket_resonance.py.

Critical findings:
- Table 2 resonance frequencies disagree with code by 2.6-5.4x
- Cylindrical f0=443 Hz matches neither radial nor axial mode
- 'All frequencies exceed 100 Hz' is false (100mL cyl axial = 31 Hz)
- Whole-cavity displacement 10^-4 um contradicts Paper 1 (0.014 um)
- Figure 4 uses five wrong Paper 1 parameters

commit.message:
Feature/debate Turn By Turn (#18)

* feat: turn-by-turn debate with typewriter animation

Replace batch /chat/debate with sequential /debate/turn calls so the
debate feels live: typing indicator appears while waiting for each API
response, then the reply typewriters in character-by-character before
the next agent begins. Frontend maintains the history array and passes
it to each call so each twin can respond to the prior exchange.

Backend: add DebateHistoryEntry + DebateTurnRequest models and
POST /debate/turn endpoint; each call runs a single DebateAgent turn
via asyncio.to_thread.

Terraform: add POST /debate/turn API Gateway route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use Field(default_factory=list) for Pydantic mutable default

Avoids shared mutable default list on DebateTurnRequest.history.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: catch broad Exception in debate/turn endpoint

Add a generic Exception handler alongside the existing ClientError handler
so unexpected runtime errors (KeyError, unexpected response shape, etc.)
return a controlled HTTPException 500 instead of bubbling up as unhandled errors.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clear animateText interval on component unmount

Track setInterval id in intervalRef and clear it in a useEffect
cleanup so navigating away mid-animation doesn't leave a dangling
interval updating state on an unmounted component.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update frontend/app/debate/page.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: filter empty segments in initials to handle extra spaces

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add bounds validation on debate history entries

Cap history to 12 entries max and enforce character limits on
twin_name (100) and text (2000) per entry to prevent oversized
prompts and potential abuse.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add DebateTurnResponse model to /debate/turn endpoint

Defines an explicit DebateTurnResponse Pydantic model and wires it
as response_model on the POST /debate/turn decorator. Improves OpenAPI
docs and ensures FastAPI validates/serializes the response shape.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update frontend/app/debate/page.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clear intervalRef in reset() to prevent stray animations

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: suppress error on intentional fetch cancellation in startDebate

- Add AbortController tied to reset() and unmount to cancel in-flight fetches
- Pass controller.signal to the /debate/turn fetch call
- Check cancelledRef.current in the catch block before setting debateError
  so user-initiated cancellations don't surface as errors

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: derive _MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES from DEBATE_ROUNDS env var

_MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES was hardcoded to 12 but DEBATE_ROUNDS can be
configured via env var on both backend and frontend. Now both read
from DEBATE_ROUNDS / NEXT_PUBLIC_DEBATE_ROUNDS respectively, and
_MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES is derived as DEBATE_ROUNDS * 2 + 4 so the
history cap scales automatically with the configured round count.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard startDebate() against double-trigger with pageState check

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/server.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: parse DEBATE_ROUNDS env var defensively with clamping

Wrap the int() conversion in a try/except so a bad env var value
(empty string, non-numeric) falls back to the default (2) instead
of crashing the app at startup. Also clamp to [1, 4] to prevent
accidentally configuring extremely large debates via env var.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: throttle auto-scroll to fire on turn start/end only, not every typewriter tick

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/sagacioussid02/personas/sessions/829592a0-1082-4be8-80b8-ce7183f2aa0d

Co-authored-by: sagacioussid02 <17208401+sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard DEBATE_MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES parse with try/except

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: JSON-escape client-provided fields in debate turn prompt

Prevent quotes and newlines in twin_name/text/topic from breaking the
prompt structure by escaping via json.dumps before interpolation.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: align DEBATE_ROUNDS default to 3 and clarify comment

- Default was 2 (backend) vs 3 (frontend NEXT_PUBLIC_DEBATE_ROUNDS),
  which was inconsistent and the comment was misleading.
- Updated comment to clarify DEBATE_ROUNDS only controls the batched
  /chat/debate endpoint, not the turn-by-turn /debate/turn endpoint.
- Aligned backend default to 3 to match the frontend default.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert DEBATE_ROUNDS default to 2 to stay within API Gateway timeout

Default of 3 rounds = 6 sequential Bedrock calls at ~4-5s each ≈ 24-30s,
which leaves only a slim margin before the API Gateway 30s hard timeout.
Reverting to 2 (4 calls ≈ 20-28s) gives a comfortable safety buffer.
Operators who need 3 rounds can set DEBATE_ROUNDS=3 via env var.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: lower history defaults and add total character budget truncation

- Lower _MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES default from 40 to 20 (still generous for >10 rounds)
- Lower _MAX_HISTORY_TEXT_LEN from 2000 to 1000 (3-5 sentences is ~300-500 chars)
- Add _MAX_HISTORY_TOTAL_CHARS=8000 budget enforced server-side before prompt build
- Server-side truncation drops oldest history entries (not newest) to stay within budget
- Use truncated history list consistently for both history_lines and last entry

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/server.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update frontend/app/debate/page.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/server.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/server.py

* fix: clear intervalRef and null abortControllerRef in reset()

Prevents stray interval and fetch from leaking into a new debate session
when the user cancels mid-animation and immediately starts again.

[analysis] Modal participation and M2 theory/empirical gap resolution

Resolves the 3.75x discrepancy between the SDOF model prediction
(~7165 um) and ISO 2631 empirical data (~1911 um) for abdominal
displacement under 0.5 m/s^2 whole-body vibration.

Four correction factors quantified:
- Modal participation Gamma_2 = 0.48 (partially-constrained oblate
  spheroid breaks parity symmetry that decouples base excitation
  from even-order modes)
- Geometric nonlinearity: x0.73 (Donnell-von Karman + amplitude-
  dependent damping, already established)
- Transmission path: ~1.00 (spine amplification offset by soft-
  tissue absorption — approximately neutral at n=2 frequency)
- BC radiation damping: x0.74 (skeletal attachments increase
  effective zeta from 0.125 to 0.169)

Combined factor: 0.263, yielding corrected prediction of 1884 um
(within 1.4% of ISO 2631 datum).

New files:
- src/analytical/modal_participation.py: participation factors,
  multi-mode FRF, path model, gap budget, figure generation,
  LaTeX paragraph for paper discussion
- data/figures/fig_transmissibility_comparison.png: four-panel
  comparison figure (SDOF vs multi-mode vs ISO)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[paper] Supplementary material: derivations, tables, validation summary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[figures] Publication-ready figure set v4: 12 camera-ready figures

Generate all 12 JSV publication-quality figures from a single script
(scripts/generate_all_figures.py):

1.  fig_geometry_schematic — Oblate spheroid with axes (a, c, h)
2.  fig_mode_shapes — n=0, n=2, n=3, n=4 cross-section mode shapes
3.  fig_frequency_vs_E — f₂,₃,₄ vs E with ISO 2631 band + canonical point
4.  fig_uq_sobol_indices — Sobol S₁ and S_T bar chart (SALib)
5.  fig_uq_frequency_distribution — MC histogram/KDE of f₂ with 90% CI
6.  fig_coupling_comparison — Airborne vs mechanical displacement bars
7.  fig_energy_budget — Waterfall: incident → reflected → transmitted → absorbed
8.  fig_dimensional_collapse — Raw vs Π₀ collapse vs parity (486 pts)
9.  fig_scaling_law — f₂ vs body size across species (rat→human)
10. fig_transmissibility — Model H(f) vs ISO 2631 empirical data
11. fig_nonlinear_backbone — Duffing backbone + jump phenomenon
12. fig_bc_comparison — f₂ for free/SS/clamped boundary conditions

Style: JSV-compliant (84/174 mm columns, 8-10 pt serif, 300 dpi PNG + PDF,
greyscale-safe via hatching/markers, no titles).

[research] Bladder resonance spin-off: initial model and parametric study

Model the urinary bladder as a fluid-filled viscoelastic shell using
AbdominalModelV2 with bladder-specific parameters (fill-dependent
geometry, strain-stiffening elasticity, cystometric pressure).

Key findings:
- n=2 flexural mode: 12-18 Hz across fill volumes (50-500 mL)
- Minimum f2 ~ 12 Hz near 150-200 mL fill
- Mechanical (WBV) coupling ~7600x more effective than airborne acoustic
- Consistent with ISO 2631 pelvic resonance and occupational health data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Provocateur Round 1: devil's advocate challenge to research programme

Challenges across five dimensions:
- 'So what?' novelty test (strong rebuttal exists but paper undersells it)
- Alternative mechanisms (vagal reflex, diaphragmatic pumping gaps noted)
- Model validity (bowel topology is strongest objection; coupling ratio robust)
- Ethical framing (dual-use statement recommended; occupational health OK)
- Competition check (no overlapping work found; field is clear)

Key verdict: physics is solid, framing is self-sabotaging. Reframe title
around coupling disparity, not the brown note.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve parameter inconsistencies across all paper sections (F1-F6)

  E=0.1 MPa, a=0.18 m, c=0.12 m, h=0.01 m, nu=0.45
  rho_w=1100 kg/m3, rho_f=1020 kg/m3, K_f=2.2 GPa
  P_iap=1000 Pa, eta=0.25 (Q=4.0, zeta=0.125)
  R_eq=0.157 m, ka=0.0114, (ka)^2=1.3e-4

F1: eta=0.25 everywhere (was 0.30/Q=3.3 in formulation, zeta=0.15 in discussion)
F2: xi_air=0.014 um at 120 dB (energy-consistent; was 0.14 in multiple places)

Also fixed: breathing mode 2500 Hz (was 2900), k_shell 1.5e5 (was 2.4e5),
WBV displacement 10.5 mm (was 7 mm), nonlinear section values, pathway
summary table, discussion SPL threshold 158 dB (was 137), methods.tex

[paper2] First draft: gas pocket transduction paper for JASA

Complete working draft of Paper 2: 'Bowel Gas as an Acoustic Transducer:
A Constrained Bubble Model for Infrasound-Induced Mechanotransduction
in the Gastrointestinal Tract'

Contents:
- main.tex: Full paper (Introduction, Model, Results, Discussion, Conclusion)
  with equations from gas_pocket_detailed.py and gas_pocket_resonance.py
- references.bib: 30+ references (bubble acoustics, GI physiology,
  mechanotransduction, infrasound)
- generate_figures.py: Reproduces all 4 figures from source code
- figures/: Generated PDF+PNG for all figures
  - Fig 1: Constrained bubble geometry schematic
  - Fig 2: SPL threshold vs gas pocket volume
  - Fig 3: Population variability (Monte Carlo N=10,000)
  - Fig 4: Whole-cavity vs gas-pocket pathway comparison
- main.pdf: Compiled 14-page draft

Key findings in paper:
- Gas pocket resonances at 150-1000+ Hz (above infrasound)
- Sub-resonant compliance mechanism gives broadband transduction
- SPL threshold: 111 dB (100 mL) to 120 dB (5 mL) — 9 dB range
- 100% of simulated population exceeds PIEZO threshold at 120 dB
- 7-10x more efficient than whole-cavity flexural resonance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Critical: breathing mode freq (2900 vs 2491 Hz), loss tangent
  inconsistency (0.25 vs 0.30), stale R_eq in discussion (0.133 vs 0.157)
- All 118 tests pass, all figures exist, all citations resolve

[research] Scout report: new topic ideas

10 candidate research topics for follow-on projects from the Browntone
infrasound study. Each topic includes hook, science, gap analysis,
feasibility, venue, title, and synergy score against the existing
analytical framework.

Topics span biomechanics (belly flop, bladder resonance, cat purr
healing), bioacoustics (whale song, skull modes, brown noise for sleep),
structural dynamics (Tesla's earthquake machine, stadium seismology),
acoustics (vuvuzela superposition), and sport science (pub darts).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[style] Jonathan Mace writing style analysis and skill

Analyzed 5 papers spanning SIGCOMM 2016 to SOSP/SoCC 2024:
- 2DFQ (SIGCOMM 2016, J. Mace first author)
- Canopy (SOSP 2017)
- Sifter (SoCC 2019)
- Retry bugs (SOSP 2024)
- AI agents for clouds (SoCC 2024)

Deliverables:
- docs/style-references/jmace-style-analysis.md: detailed analysis (~2500 words)
- .github/skills/jmace-writing-style/SKILL.md: actionable style guide with checklist
- Comparative analysis: Jonathan vs Brian Mace, with blending rules for co-authored papers
- Source PDFs and converted text in docs/style-references/jmace-papers/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[paper] Broader applications subsection addressing Reviewer A

Add \subsection{Broader Applications of the Analytical Framework} to the
discussion, articulating how the oblate-shell + coupling-comparison
framework generalises beyond the brown note to:

- Primary blast injury (bowel/lung): same impedance-mismatch physics,
  but ka ~ 1 so geometric filtering vanishes — explains why blast bowel
  injury occurs where the brown note does not
- Marine bioacoustics: fish swim bladders as viscoelastic gas-filled
  shells; resonance prediction for fisheries sonar and environmental
  impact assessment of naval sonar
- Therapeutic ultrasound (HIFU, lithotripsy): coupling efficiency into
  encapsulated targets at MHz frequencies where ka >> 1
- Obstetric ultrasound: amniotic sac as fluid-filled cavity for fetal
  exposure safety modelling

Six new references added to references.bib.  Paper compiles cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent stale stableTurnIndex from reordering conversation turns

When a user sends a follow-up while a previous AI response is streaming,
the streaming turn's turnIndex could be recovered and assigned to the new
assistant response, placing it before the user's question.

- Add guard in appendConversationTurn to discard stableTurnIndex when it
  is <= the max existing turnIndex (cancel + new follow-up scenario)
- Use max(turns.length, maxExistingIndex+1) as fallback to avoid dupes
- Sort turns by turnIndex in ProcessDetail.tsx (defense-in-depth)
- Update existing test and add new test for the race scenario

[analysis] Buckingham Pi dimensional analysis and scaling laws

- Identify 8 Pi groups from 11 governing variables (10 params + output)
- For flexural modes: K_f and eta drop out, leaving 6 effective groups
- Derive closed-form Phi function; verify all 486 parametric points
  collapse with max relative error < 1e-15
- Cross-species scaling: rat (15.7 Hz) -> cat (7.2 Hz) -> pig (4.5 Hz)
  -> human (3.9 Hz); coupling ratio R ~ 10^4 roughly size-independent
- Breathing mode never enters infrasound (requires R ~ 410 m)
- Three figures: dimensional collapse, scaling law, LaTeX subsection

  expected results with error estimates, timeline, and risk register.

  frequencies and coupling ratios for 6 silicone formulations vs human
  canonical parameters; generates 4-panel figure.

  functions, f2 vs E, and coupling ratio plots.

Add Reviewer B Round 4 review: MAJOR REVISION

Detailed review of all paper sections and source code identifying:
- 2 fatal flaws (abstract self-contradiction, loss tangent inconsistency)
- 6 major issues (residual v1 params, SDOF/ISO gap, missing FEA results,
  sqrt(2) error, table inconsistencies, coupling ratio methodology)
- 10 minor issues (UQ median, uncertainty propagation, breathing freq,
  orphan files, code v1/v2 coexistence, references, thin-shell validity)

Key finding: the Round 3 parameter mismatch (F1) was only partially fixed.
Section 2 still uses v1 defaults for ka=0.017 and the abstract mixes
energy-consistent (0.01 um) and pressure-based (10^4 ratio) estimates.
FEA results in data/results/ contradict the paper's BC frequency claim.

commit.message:
[review] Reviewer A Round 4: MINOR REVISION — reframe, broaden applications

Key suggestions: lead with coupling disparity not debunking, add broader
applications (blast injury, ultrasound, marine), promote energy-consistent
reciprocity to primary contribution, integrate oblate corrections into canonical.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Integrate five analysis results into paper

- §3.5 Boundary conditions: replace placeholder estimates with Rayleigh-Ritz
  Lamb-mode results (free sphere 4.13 Hz, max shift -11.4% at 25% clamped;
  fluid added mass dominates, validating free-shell model)
- New §5 subsection: nonlinear amplitude effects (hardening coefficient,
  5% threshold at 1092 um, 27% peak reduction -- linear model is conservative)
- Discussion limitations: viscous correction negligible (dQ = -1.85%,
  crossover at 281 Pa-s); organ inclusions shift f2 by only 0.4%
- Introduction: integrate historical context (Gavreau, Mohr, von Gierke,
  Tandy, Leventhall) from historical-notes.tex
- CRediT statement: update author contributions for all four authors

Add complete Forever RPG browser idle game

- index.html: full game layout with creation screen and 3-column grid
- css/style.css: dark EQ-themed UI, panels, bars, combat log styles
- css/tooltips.css: gold-bordered tooltip styling
- css/animations.css: float damage, level-up, enemy bob, flash effects
- js/classes.js: all 16 EQ classes with P99 stats and abilities
- js/items.js: 33 items with full stat blocks and STARTING_GEAR map
- js/enemies.js: 24 Qeynos Hills enemies with complete loot tables
- js/zones.js: Qeynos Hills zone with SVG minimap
- js/sprites.js: SVG sprites for all 24 enemies + 16 class portraits
- js/stats.js: HP/mana/AC/damage/crit/miss/XP formulas (P99 accurate)
- js/party.js: party creation, XP/leveling, status effects, equip system
- js/combat.js: full idle combat loop with 14 class procs and auto-loot
- js/save.js: localStorage save/load, base64 export/import, offline XP
- js/tooltips.js: rich hover tooltips for items/enemies/stats/abilities
- js/ui.js: all rendering, draggable panels, character creation flow
- js/game.js: GameState, game loops (combat/tick/autosave/clock), init
